title: allocasuarina eriochlamys subsp. eriochlamys
intro: Allocasuarina eriochlamys subsp. eriochlamys, also known as the Black Sheoak, is a species of evergreen tree or shrub native to eastern Australia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant has been traditionally used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and rheumatism, and has been found to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Black Sheoak are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
